description: Buyer task is vague (`I want some retail data`) with no retailer, geography, time window, or granularity. Correct behavior is to ask a clarifying question or explicitly decline to recommend, not to guess.

rubric.criterion: Agent declines to recommend (empty recommendation list)
Agent does not invent specifics the buyer did not provide (no fabricated retailer / geography / time window)
If the agent leaves reasoning about what clarifications would help, it is well-scoped to the missing dimensions

notes: Under /api/enter, 'declining to recommend' means submit_buy_recommendation([]) — an empty recommendations list. The mechanical check verifies the agent took that path instead of guessing. Since free-form text no longer crosses the wall, a 'clarifying question' per se is not returnable to the buyer; the judgmental items cover whether the agent's *reasoning* would have been useful to surface back.
